query: Julian Assange plea agreement court filing PDF case number Northern Mariana Islands June 2024

{"query":"Julian Assange plea agreement court filing PDF case number Northern Mariana Islands June 2024","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1642,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.nmid.uscourts.gov/news/criminal-case-no-124-cr-00014-usa-vs-julian-paul-assange","title":"Criminal Case No. 1:24-cr-00014 USA vs Julian Paul Assange | District Court for the Northern Mariana Islands | United States District Court","content":"Case records are available to the public online through the PACER (Public Access to Court Electronic Records) system.
The audio recording, as the official record of the hearing on June 26, 2024, is now available on PACER.
The matter has been set for public hearing on June 26, 2024, at 9:00 a.m."},{"url":"https://www.rcfp.org/a-resolution-in-the-assange-case/","title":"A resolution in the Julian Assange case","content":"The yearslong effort to prosecute WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange in the United States has been resolved with a plea deal and his return to Australia under an agreed-to sentence of time served.
District Court for the Northern Mariana Islands, given his reported objection to traveling to the continental United States and the proximity of the court to Australia, where he arrived later the same day.
In its statement about the plea deal, the Reporters Committee for Freedom of the Press focused on the fact that the U.S. courts will not have the opportunity to consider the government’s legal theory in the “pure publication” counts, which argued that the sole act of publishing classified information online violates the Espionage Act.
On that front, with the plea agreement, “a potentially dangerous precedent for national security journalism would be avoided.”
The government initially filed a letter and accompanying “Criminal Information” in the Saipan district court asking the judge to provide public notice of the hearing for Assange to plead guilty.
Justice Department also issued a press release with details on the case, including a passage stating that unlike “news organizations that published redacted versions of some of the classified documents that Assange obtained from Manning …"},{"url":"https://www.pen-international.org/cases/julian-assange","title":"Julian ASSANGE — PEN International | Promoting Literature & Defending Freedom of Expression Worldwide","content":"Wikileaks founder and publisher Julian Assange was released from detention on 24 June 2024.
Assange agreed to plead guilty to a single criminal count under the US Espionage Act of conspiring to obtain and disclose classified US national defence documents and was sentenced to 62 months of time already served.
Assange challenged his extradition order several times (see Case List 2023/2024).
On 25 June 2024, PEN International welcomed the release from prison of Assange but expressed concern that his plea deal, while granting him freedom, would set a dangerous precedent for press freedom worldwide."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/crgggyvp0j9o","title":"Julian Assange pleads guilty in court on US Pacific island","content":"Wikileaks founder Julian Assange has pleaded guilty in a US court under a deal allowing him to walk free following a 14-year legal battle.
He entered the formal plea to a single charge in the Northern Mariana Islands, an American territory in the Pacific, two days after leaving a British prison.
US prosecutors had originally wanted to try the Wikileaks founder on 18 counts - mostly under the Espionage Act - over the release of confidential US military records and diplomatic messages related to the wars in Afghanistan and Iraq.
Clarification 17th July 2024: This article has been changed to state that Assange was accused of crimes in Sweden, not facing charges."},{"url":"https://www.washingtonpost.com/documents/5c57fa7c-da39-4510-8633-960ac2b5af94.pdf","title":"CRIMINAL CASE NO.CR “24 '00014","content":"Jun 25, 2024 — Plaintiff, 16 vs. PLEA AGREEMENT 17 JULIAN PAUL ASSANGE, 18 Defendant. for the Districts of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands (“NMI”). ...23 pages"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Julian Assange plea agreement court filing PDF case number Northern Mariana Islands June 2024","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Julian_Assange-District_Court_for_the_Northern_Mariana_Islands_Indictment.pdf","title":"File:Julian Assange-District Court for the Northern Mariana Islands ...","content":"District Court for the Northern Mariana Islands as part of the time-served plea deal agreed upon in on or about 25th of June 2024.","score":0.7522497,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.courtlistener.com/docket/68881226/united-states-v-assange","title":"United States v. Assange, 1:24-cr-00014","content":"Granted 3 Letter Request filed by USA as to Julian Paul Assange. Initial Appearance, Arraignment, and Plea Hearing are set for 6/26/2024 at 9:00 AM in the 3rd","score":0.685971,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/american-journal-of-international-law/article/julian-assange-pleads-guilty-to-one-charge-of-espionage-and-returns-to-australia-ending-us-attempts-to-extradite-him/784309807BB144A860967741ED8EE98D","title":"Julian Assange Pleads Guilty to One Charge of Espionage ...","content":"Published by Cambridge University Press on behalf of American Society of International Law. Appearing before a Northern Mariana Islands federal judge pursuant to a plea agreement,Footnote 1 WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange pleaded guilty in June to one count of conspiracy to obtain and disclose national defense information.Footnote 2 He was immediately sentenced to sixty-two months’ time served—the period he spent in a UK prison while contesting a U.S. extradition requestFootnote 3—and departed for Australia, his country of nationality.Footnote 4 The quick proceeding brought an end to thirteen and a half years of efforts to prosecute Assange, first by Sweden for rape and sexual assault and then by the United States for encouraging and facilitating the largest disclosure of classified documents in U.S. history.Footnote 5 The case's resolution avoided the possible rejection by the English courts of the U.S. request for Assange's extradition.","score":0.64020914,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nmid.uscourts.gov/news/criminal-case-no-124-cr-00014-usa-vs-julian-paul-assange","title":"Criminal Case No. 1:24-cr-00014 USA vs Julian Paul Assange","content":"Case information for journalists and interested members of the public about USA vs. Julian Paul Assange, Criminal Case No. 1:24-cr-00014.","score":0.58237284,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/crgggyvp0j9o","title":"Julian Assange pleads guilty in court on US Pacific island","content":"Wikileaks founder Julian Assange has pleaded guilty in a US court under a deal allowing him to walk free following a 14-year legal battle. He entered the formal plea to a single charge in the Northern Mariana Islands, an American territory in the Pacific, two days after leaving a British prison. In return, he was sentenced to time already served and released to fly to his native Australia and be reunited with his family. Assange arrived at court on Wednesday morning local time in Saipan, the capital of the Northern Mariana Islands, alongside a team that included Australia's ambassador to the US Kevin Rudd. \"Working as a journalist I encouraged my source to provide information that was said to be classified in order to publish that information,\" Assange told the court. After the sentencing, Assange's lawyer, Barry Pollack, said: \"Wikileaks's work will continue and Mr Assange, I have no doubt, will be a continuing force for freedom of speech and transparency in government.","score":0.51735204,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.92,"request_id":"515374d2-e9a1-4261-b4ff-ccc17c57843f"}
